summary: Matched src/melee/lb/lbcardgame.c and moved it from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The fix was mostly small C-shape and data-shape work: fully spelling out lb_803BAB74's static initializer, adjusting fields from UNK_T to u32, changing the OS tick-to-seconds conversion expression, using a chained assignment in lb_8001CC84, correcting a struct offset comment, and updating GALE01 symbols for the emitted data/string layout.
what_changed: lb_803BAB74's anonymous static struct initialization was expanded from a placeholder zero initializer to explicit values: pad {0, 3, 0, 0x1790, 0}, NULL x14, seven repeated {0x1F2C, 1, NULL} entries, and a sentinel {-1, 0, NULL}.
The first two fields of lb_803BAB74.unk_arr entries changed from UNK_T to u32, giving the initializer concrete integer types.

lb_8001C658 now computes seconds with OSTicksToSeconds(temp_r6), stores it in a u32, then calls OSTicksToCalendarTime(OSSecondsToTicks((u64) seconds), &time) instead of dividing OSGetTime() by OS_TIMER_CLOCK directly.
lb_8001CC84 removed an unused local var_r3 and changed lb_80433318.x10 = 0 to lb_80433318.x10 = temp_r3 = 0, likely to match generated code shape.
lbcardgame.static.h corrected the offset comment for lb_80433318_t._1C from +30 to +1C without changing the field layout.
config/GALE01/symbols.txt renamed several lbArchive/lb/string symbols to compiler-style @NN labels and adjusted lb_803BAB74's data size from 0x28 to 0x78, consistent with the expanded static object.
smart_moves: Solved matching by making the static data object explicit rather than hiding it behind a placeholder initializer.
Used concrete u32 fields in the nested array where integer constants were being emitted, reducing ambiguity from UNK_T and helping data/code generation match.
Preserved SDK time-conversion semantics through OSTicksToSeconds and OSSecondsToTicks while forcing the intermediate width with u32 and u64 casts.
Used a chained assignment to make lb_80433318.x10 and temp_r3 receive zero together, a small source-level expression change that can affect register reuse and instruction ordering.
Updated symbols.txt alongside the source match so the data sections and local string labels reflected the newly matched object output.
decomp_lessons: When a file is blocked by data mismatch, inspect static initializers and object sizes; a placeholder initializer can hide the real emitted data footprint.
Replacing UNK_T with a concrete scalar type can be necessary for matching static data and expression codegen, especially when constants are known.

For time APIs, equivalent arithmetic such as ticks / OS_TIMER_CLOCK may not match the compiler output produced by SDK macros/functions; prefer the original-looking SDK conversion chain when evidence supports it.
Chained assignments can be a legitimate matching tactic for preserving a temporary's value and register lifetime.
Offset-comment fixes in static headers matter for future readers even when they do not alter the binary layout.
Symbols may need to move from guessed subsystem-prefixed names to @NN compiler/local labels once source emits data in the expected order.
naming_conventions: Compiler-generated or local literal/data labels in symbols.txt may use @NN names such as @12, @13, @162, @334, and @336 instead of guessed lbArchive_ or lb_ address-style names.
Unidentified globals and structs continued to use address-based names such as lb_803BAB74 and lb_80433318_t.
Struct field names retained offset-style placeholders such as x10, x14, x5C, and _1C where semantic names were not established.
The PR did not provide human discussion explaining naming intent, so naming lessons are inferred from the diff only.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Expanded lb_803BAB74 to a 0x78-byte initialized data object, aligning source-level initialization with symbols.txt size changes.
Typed nested initializer fields as u32 instead of UNK_T to influence emitted data and expression typing.
Changed tick conversion from manual division by OS_TIMER_CLOCK to OSTicksToSeconds plus OSSecondsToTicks with explicit u32/u64 widths.
Used lb_80433318.x10 = temp_r3 = 0 to force codegen around zeroing both a struct field and a local temporary.
Removed an unused local variable in lb_8001CC84 after the matching expression no longer needed it.
Renamed/merged emitted strings and data labels in symbols.txt, including replacing several named string labels with @NN labels.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
One issue comment was counted, but its body was not included in the excerpt, so no evidence-grounded reviewer feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find other lb files where placeholder static initializers are preventing object matches.
Search for manual OSGetTime division by OS_TIMER_CLOCK that should use OSTicksToSeconds/OSSecondsToTicks for matching.
Search for UNK_T fields inside initialized static data that can be narrowed to u32 or another concrete scalar type.
Review symbols.txt entries near lbcardgame for remaining guessed lb_ or subsystem-prefixed names that should become @NN labels.
Look for cases where chained assignment can reproduce register lifetime in small state-machine functions.
